edges.description: SRD5A2 loss of function lowers 5-alpha-reductase type 2 enzymatic activity.
Reduced enzyme activity causes deficient testosterone-to-DHT conversion.
Impaired testosterone-to-DHT conversion reduces DHT production.
Reduced DHT relative to testosterone increases the diagnostic T/DHT ratio.
Impaired testosterone-to-dihydrotestosterone conversion is represented as a steroid metabolism/homeostasis abnormality.
Impaired testosterone-to-DHT conversion reduces DHT-dependent fetal masculinization signals.
The persistent DHT-production defect explains undervirilization at birth, while pubertal testosterone exposure can still drive virilization through androgen receptor signaling.
intermediate_mechanisms: normal pubertal testosterone production with preserved androgen receptor signaling

edges.description: Fetal DHT deficiency causes variable 46,XY undervirilization, including ambiguous genitalia, perineal hypospadias, small or bifid scrotum, and micropenis.
Fetal DHT deficiency is the core endocrine-metabolic abnormality in this androgen metabolism disorder.
Undervirilization includes undescended testes.
Variable undervirilization can produce ambiguous genitalia in a 46,XY individual.
DHT-dependent scrotal development is reduced in the undervirilization phenotype.
Incomplete labioscrotal fusion is part of the undervirilization spectrum.
Urethral masculinization can be incomplete, producing perineal hypospadias.
External genital appearance may be ambiguous or female appearing.
Genital tract anatomy and maldescent can reduce fertility in affected 46,XY individuals.
intermediate_mechanisms: abnormal testicular position and genital tract development

edges.description: DHT-dependent phallic growth is reduced, producing micropenis or penile hypoplasia.
DHT-dependent phallic growth is reduced, producing micropenis.
Severe undervirilization can preserve urogenital sinus anatomy.
Testosterone exposure at puberty contributes to virilization despite reduced DHT synthesis.
Pubertal androgen exposure produces the reported virilization phenotype.
Pubertal virilization can include deepening of the voice.
Lower DHT reports the deficient SRD5A2-dependent product.
Higher T/DHT ratio reports impaired conversion of testosterone to DHT.

nodes.description: The clinical phenotype ranges from female-appearing external genitalia to ambiguous genitalia, perineoscrotal hypospadias, bifid or small scrotum, micropenis, cryptorchidism, urogenital sinus anatomy, and reduced fertility.
